title: fraxinus excelsior subsp. syriaca
intro: Fraxinus excelsior subsp. syriaca, also known as the Syrian ash, is a subspecies of the European ash tree (Fraxinus excelsior), which is a deciduous tree native to Europe and parts of Asia. Traditionally, the bark, leaves, and flowers of the Syrian ash have been used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Fraxinus excelsior subsp. syriaca include its ability to reduce fever, soothe skin irritations, and act as a natural antihistamine, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for allergies and skin conditions. Fraxinus excelsior subsp. syriaca can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
